question: Which Android layout is the default in modern Android Studio templates?
description: ConstraintLayout is the default in Android Studio. It positions views using constraints relative to other views or the parent.

question: Which orientation values are valid for LinearLayout?
description: android:orientation in LinearLayout accepts only vertical or horizontal.

question: What does layout_width="match_parent" mean?
option_a: The view shrinks to fit its content
option_b: The view fills the parent's width
option_c: The view takes a fixed size in dp
description: match_parent tells the view to take up all the available width (or height) of its parent container.

question: What does layout_width="wrap_content" mean?
option_b: The view takes only the space its content needs
description: wrap_content sizes the view just large enough to contain its content, no extra space.

question: What is the purpose of layout_weight in LinearLayout?
option_a: To set the view's font weight
option_b: To distribute remaining space proportionally between children
description: layout_weight distributes the remaining space between LinearLayout children proportionally. A child with weight 2 gets twice the leftover space of one with weight 1.

question: When using layout_weight horizontally, what should layout_width usually be set to?
description: Set layout_width="0dp" when using horizontal weights — this lets the LinearLayout distribute width purely by weight without considering content size.

question: Which layout stacks all its children on top of each other (in z-order)?
description: FrameLayout stacks children in z-order — useful for overlays or single-image containers.

question: Which element is used to define a row inside a TableLayout?
description: TableRow represents a single row in a TableLayout. Each child of a TableRow becomes a cell in that row.

question: Which TableLayout attribute makes a column stretch to fill available space?
description: android:stretchColumns makes the specified column(s) stretch to take up any leftover horizontal space.

question: Which attribute hides (collapses) a column in TableLayout?
description: android:collapseColumns hides the specified column(s) — the column becomes invisible and takes no space.

question: In TableLayout, what does layout_span do for a child view?
option_b: Specifies how many columns the cell spans (like HTML colspan)
description: layout_span defines the number of columns a cell should occupy — equivalent to colspan in HTML tables.

question: Which attribute on a TableRow child specifies the starting column index?
description: layout_column tells the cell which column to start in — useful for skipping columns or aligning cells.

question: Which attribute of ImageView sets the image source compatible with AppCompat?
description: app:srcCompat is the AppCompat-friendly attribute for setting an ImageView's source — supports vector drawables on older Android versions.

question: Where are layout XML files stored in an Android project?
description: All layout XML files live in res/layout/ (e.g. activity_main.xml).

question: Which file must declare every Activity in an Android app?
description: Every Activity must be declared in AndroidManifest.xml inside <application>. Otherwise the system can't find or launch it.

question: Which intent-filter combination marks an Activity as the app's launcher entry point?
description: An Activity becomes the launcher entry point when its intent-filter contains action.MAIN + category.LAUNCHER. This makes it appear in the app drawer.

question: What does android:exported="true" mean on an Activity?
option_a: The Activity is published to the Play Store
option_b: Other apps can launch this Activity
option_c: The Activity is exported as a library
option_d: The Activity runs in the background
description: exported="true" allows other apps (or the system launcher) to start this Activity. Required since Android 12 for any Activity with an intent-filter.

question: When you create a new Empty Activity in Android Studio, what does it generate?
option_c: A Java/Kotlin class + XML layout + manifest entry
description: Creating an Empty Activity generates: a Java/Kotlin class, an XML layout (e.g. activity_xxx.xml), and registers the activity in AndroidManifest.xml.

question: Which View is used to display non-editable text on the screen?
description: TextView displays read-only text. EditText is the editable input field for user typing.

question: Which View is used to accept text input from the user (e.g., username/password)?
description: EditText is the input field for user-typed text. Common attributes: hint, inputType, id.
